title: Buttery Algerian Dates Quesadilla
description: A whole30-friendly, crispy and buttery dish perfect for date night with a touch of maple syrup sweetness.

ingredients.name: Whole wheat tortilla
ingredients.notes: Can be used as gluten-free if needed.

ingredients.name: Buttery almond flour (or all-purpose flour for gluten-free)
ingredients.notes: For a nutty flavor, use almond flour instead of plain.

ingredients.name: Dates
ingredients.notes: Choose pitted and diced dates to make the dough easier.

ingredients.name: Maple syrup
ingredients.notes: For a touch of sweetness, reduce if too sweet for taste preference.

ingredients.name: Almond extract (vanilla can be used as an alternative)

instructions: Preheat broiler and set oven rack to the highest position.
Mix almond flour with dates until a dough forms. Add maple syrup and cumin (if using), mix well, then add salt and knead until fully incorporated.
Divide dough into 2-3 equal parts for each quesadilla and shape them gently onto tortillas.
Brush tops of the quesadillas lightly with olive oil or melted coconut oil to prevent sticking. Place on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper if using.
Broil in preheated oven until edges start to brown, about 3-4 minutes per side. Watch carefully as they can burn quickly.
Remove from heat and serve hot.
